title: Timeline item grouping utility for read and grep operations
purpose: Groups consecutive read and grep tool calls into timeline groups when they share common directory prefixes, improving visualization of related file operations.
module_context: Exports TimelineGroup and GroupedTimelineItem types, and a groupTimeline function that processes agent timeline items into grouped structures.

steps.content: Handle empty input by returning null.
Split each path into segments using forward slashes.
Compare segments across all paths to find the longest common prefix.
Drop the last segment of the prefix to ensure only directory components are included.
Return null if the resulting prefix is empty or just the root directory.
Return the joined prefix string if it contains meaningful directory components.
Verify the item is a tool call type.
Check that the tool call name is 'Read'.
Validate that the input contains a file_path property as a string.
Return true if all conditions are met, otherwise false.
Verify the item is a tool call type.
Check that the tool call name is 'Grep'.
Return true if the name matches, otherwise false.
Map each item to extract its file path from the input.
Filter out any empty paths from the results.
Return the array of valid file paths.
Initialize an output array and a running buffer for grouping.
Define a flush function to handle ending a group and adding it to the output.
Process each item in the input timeline.
For read tool calls, determine if they can be grouped with the current run.
If a group can be formed, add the item to the current run buffer.
If grouping is not possible, flush the current run and start a new one.
For grep tool calls, check if they can be grouped with the current run.
If grouping is possible, add the item to the current run buffer.
If grouping is not possible, flush the current run and start a new one.
For non-tool call items, flush the current run and add the item directly to output.
Flush any remaining items in the current run after processing all input.
Return the final grouped timeline items.
Check if there is an active run to flush.
If the run buffer contains three or more items, create a group.
For read groups, compute and attach the common directory prefix.
Add the group to the output array.
If the run buffer contains fewer than three items, add each item individually to the output.
Reset the current run to null.
